title: Thirsty Bean
prompt: Which bean will grow tallest after a week?

options: No water but lots of light ☀️🚫
Lots of water but in dark cupboard 🌑💧

explanation: Plants need both water and light to make food and grow tall.
funFact: A bean seed carries a tiny packed lunch to start growing!

title: Sun Seeker
prompt: Where should we put the plant to help it grow best?
explanation: Light gives plants energy to make food. No light means no growth.
funFact: Plants lean towards light — try it with cress on a windowsill!

title: Flower Doctor
prompt: Sort these flower parts into the right groups
explanation: Sepals protect, petals attract, stamen makes pollen, stigma catches it for seeds.
funFact: Bees can see ultraviolet patterns on petals we cannot!

title: Celery Straw
prompt: How long to see blue water reach the celery leaves?
explanation: Water travels up tiny tubes called xylem in the stem, like drinking straws.
funFact: You can split a celery stalk and put each half in different colours!

title: Seed Flinger
prompt: How does a dandelion seed travel?
explanation: Dandelion seeds have fluffy parachutes to float on wind far away.
funFact: Some seeds can fly over 30 kilometres on wind!

title: Burr Stick
prompt: How does a burdock burr spread?
explanation: Burrs have tiny hooks that cling to fur or socks for a free ride.
funFact: Velcro was invented after looking at burdock burrs under a microscope!

title: Life Cycle Sort
prompt: Put the plant life cycle in order
explanation: Seed → sprout → seedling → adult plant that makes flowers and new seeds.
funFact: The oldest seed ever grown was 2,000 years old — a date palm from Israel!

title: Space Check
prompt: What else do plants need besides water light air?
explanation: Plants need nutrients from soil and room for roots and leaves to spread.
funFact: Too many plants crowded together compete and stay small, like in a busy playground!

title: Flower Parts
prompt: Which part attracts insects with bright colours and scent?
explanation: Petals are bright and scented to attract pollinators like bees.
funFact: Some flowers smell like rotten meat to attract flies!

title: Pollination Movers
prompt: How does pollen travel from flower to flower most often?
explanation: Insects like bees carry pollen, wind blows it, animals brush past.
funFact: One bee can visit 5,000 flowers in a single day!

title: Seed Ways
prompt: Sort seed dispersal methods to examples
explanation: Seeds use wind, hooks, explosion, water, or being eaten to travel far from parent plant.
funFact: Coconuts can float across oceans and still sprout on new beaches!

title: What Plants Need Check
prompt: Which is NOT needed for healthy plant growth?
explanation: Plants need light water air nutrients space room temperature. Music does not help.
funFact: Talking to plants is kind but light matters more than sound!
